_meta.vertical: Residential roofing (replacement, storm/hail restoration, insurance claim)
_meta.audience: Roofing company owner-operator + homeowner (B2B + B2C mix depending on call list)
_meta.source_note: Aggregated from large outbound call-data studies (millions of calls) plus residential home-services nuances. B2B roofer-to-roofer calls follow more standard business hours.

core_windows.label: Late morning sweet spot
core_windows.note: 9–10 a.m. is the strongest hour. Roofing owners are usually in the office or truck after the morning crew dispatch but before the first jobsite walk.

core_windows.label: Late afternoon wrap-up
core_windows.note: Owners are off the jobsite, back at the truck, processing the day. Highest engagement window.

experimental_blocks.label: Owner cool-down
experimental_blocks.note: Roofing owners who took crew calls all day are most receptive after 5:30 p.m.

experimental_blocks.label: Saturday morning
experimental_blocks.note: Single-truck and small-shop owners often work Saturdays. Verify TCPA / state rules.

avoid_windows.label: Crew dispatch chaos
avoid_windows.reason: Roofing owners are loading trucks and dispatching crews — terrible time to ring.

avoid_windows.label: Jobsite walk window
avoid_windows.reason: Mid-day jobsite walks + lunch. Lowest connect rate.

avoid_days.reason: Crew scheduling chaos + worst connect-rate day in most studies.
Hit-or-miss; owners doing payroll + Friday close-outs.
Respect family time; very weak engagement for roofers.

homeowner_nuances: Roofing is seasonal — answer rate and engagement spike 3-10 days after a hail or wind event in the prospect's metro. Pull NOAA/SPC alerts and prioritize storm-affected lists.
Owner-operators answer their own cells. Mid-size companies (5+ trucks) usually have a gatekeeper between 9-5 — call before 9 a.m. or after 5 p.m. to reach the owner directly.
Hail-belt markets (DFW, OKC, Denver, Houston, Phoenix) have higher CPCs ($35-$80) and storm-week CPC spikes — call when ad-spend pain is freshest.
Insurance-claim narrative resonates strongest in TX, OK, CO, KS, FL, NC. Reference {leak_2} as the missing claim-buyer language.
